introduction: This presentation provides a comprehensive analysis of the IT service requests addressed by our team in May 2025. The purpose is to review our performance and identify areas for improvement.

subsections.heading: Breakdown of IT Service Request Types
subsections.content:
• Software-related service requests made up 42% of the total in 2025
• Hardware issues accounted for 35% of service requests
• Network connectivity problems constituted 15% of service requests
• The remaining 8% of service requests were miscellaneous, including data retrieval and security concerns
• Software issues were primarily related to application updates and bug fixes
• Hardware problems were mostly related to workstation and peripheral malfunctions
• Network connectivity issues were typically related to Wi-Fi and VPN access
subsections.justification: This content provides a breakdown of IT service request types, but does not specify the total number of requests in May 2025. It uses a distractor strategy by focusing on the nature of IT service requests rather than their number.

subsections.heading: Resolution Time for IT Service Requests in May 2025
subsections.content:
• The average resolution time for software-related issues was 1.2 hours
• Hardware-related problems had an average resolution time of 2.5 hours
• Network connectivity problems were typically resolved in 0.8 hours
• Miscellaneous service requests had an average resolution time of 1.5 hours
• The longest resolution time in May was 8 hours for a complex hardware issue
• The quickest resolution was a network issue fixed in 15 minutes
• The IT & Security team aims to keep the average resolution time below 2 hours for all request types
subsections.justification: This slide presents data on resolution times for May 2025, but it does not provide the total number of IT service requests addressed in that month. The distractor strategy here is focusing on performance metrics instead of volume metrics.

subsections.heading: Total IT Service Requests Handled in May 2025
subsections.content:
• The IT & Security team has seen a consistent increase in service requests year-over-year
• The busiest day of the week for service requests is Monday
• The least busy day for service requests is Friday
• The majority of service requests are submitted between 10 AM and 2 PM
• The fewest service requests are submitted between 6 PM and 6 AM
• The team is considering staff reallocation to better handle peak times
• Software-related service requests have shown the most significant increase over the years
subsections.justification: This information discusses trends and patterns in the IT service request volume, but does not reveal the total number of requests handled in May 2025. The distractor strategy is focusing on general trends and patterns.

conclusion: Analysis of the IT service requests in May shows our team's efficiency in handling and resolving issues. Going forward, we will focus on areas identified for improvement to enhance our IT service delivery.
